codeReview.summary: The CI step-label cleanup in commit 2cf050e is correct, but the current repository state for this feature still does not fully satisfy the workflow-fix intent or VAL-PLATFORM-003/004 because the committed docs/workflow truth sources remain inconsistent and one committed helper cannot actually start the documented port safely on Windows.

issues.description: The env template still contains duplicated, partially conflicting header/comment blocks. This leaves contributor docs out of sync with the claimed aligned CI + contributor docs workflow and violates the feature requirement to keep committed workflow/docs truthful and clean.

The committed Windows helper sets PORT=3005 and then runs pnpm run dev, but package.json hardcodes next dev -p 3005, so if port 3005 is already occupied the script cannot choose another port within the agreed range and may fail immediately. This helper does not reliably deliver the stated repo-runs-cleanly-on-Windows-in-range behavior.

The current README in repository state contains only the project title and disclaimer, so contributor docs do not document the chosen mission port or the committed lint/static-check, typecheck, test, and build workflow as required by VAL-PLATFORM-003/004 and the feature description.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The platform-docs-worker skill says workflow features should read .factory/library/*.md, verify current failure modes first, and make the smallest coherent set of edits. The reviewed transcript for session d8fa56f8-474e-410c-8cb9-ef62002d5571 only touched CI/workflow files and did not show reads of the library files before the edit; meanwhile earlier sessions for this feature created ad hoc batch helpers and left broader truth gaps behind. The skill may need stronger guidance to re-check the whole feature surface, not just the latest diff, when fixing a partial workflow feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for d8fa56f8-474e-410c-8cb9-ef62002d5571 shows only CI/workflow-focused checks, while current repo state still has README and .env.example truth gaps relative to feature intent. Skill procedure step 1 requires reading .factory/library/*.md; this session transcript does not evidence that step.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state documents Windows as the platform and .factory/services.yaml as the source of truth, but it does not capture that helper batch files (start-dev-3005.bat, healthcheck-3005.bat, stop-dev-3005.bat, ci-validate.bat) were introduced during partial workflow attempts and are not required by the validation contract. A cleanup/usage note in shared state would help prevent future workers from treating these ad hoc wrappers as mandatory platform truth.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Current repo root contains four workflow batch files alongside .factory/services.yaml; the validation contract cites package.json, docs, CI, and command evidence, not these wrappers.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, commit 2cf050e, and the current repository files touched by the workflow fix. The CI label fix itself is correct, but the overall feature still fails review because contributor truth sources remain inconsistent: README does not document the workflow at all, .env.example is still messy/duplicative, and the checked-in Windows start helper is not robust for the claimed mission-port workflow.
